title: Testimony of officer who shot Kelsey Fitzsimmons continues on day 2 of trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial proceeds at Essex County Superior Court for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
Officer Patrick Noonan testified about the June 2025 incident, stating he was in 'complete shock' when Fitzsimmons pulled a gun.
Judge Jeffrey Karp plans to conduct a site visit to the North Andover home where the shooting occurred later this week.

title: After years of illness, Natick woman runs Boston Marathon with BAA
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Mackenzie Roy, a Natick resident, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon after overcoming autoimmune diseases.
Roy is participating in the marathon through the Boston Athletic Association's B.A.A. Gives Back program.
She grew up with a family tradition of watching the Boston Marathon and was inspired to eventually participate.

title: Bruins’ top prospect James Hagens welcomes challenge as he begins pro career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins' prospect James Hagens begins his pro career with AHL's Providence team after leaving Boston College.
Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement and will debut against the Springfield Thunderbirds.
Providence coach Ryan Mougenel emphasizes Hagens' development in the AHL to prepare for a future NHL role.

title: What Joe Mazzulla and Derrick White said about their Monday Night Raw appearance
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla and Derrick White attended WWE's Monday Night Raw at TD Garden for family fun and learning.
Mazzulla and White shared playful interactions, with White joking about a potential WrestleMania rematch.
Mazzulla emphasized learning from top performers in different arenas and relationship-building off the court.

title: Comella’s closes Melrose location after 13 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Comella’s has closed its Melrose location after 13 years due to operational and financial challenges.
The closure, confirmed by MassLive, was a franchisee-level decision affecting the 478A Main St. restaurant.
Comella’s continues operating 10 other sites in Massachusetts, including locations in Belmont, Concord, and Natick.

title: The most underrated movies of 2025 are streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Naked Gun' stars Liam Neeson and Pamela Anderson in a comedy with a script full of jokes, streaming on Paramount+.
'The Ballad of Wallis Island' features Tim Key as a lottery winner reuniting a folk duo, available on Prime Video.
'28 Years Later' sequel explores Brexit themes through a zombie hunt, starring Alfie Williams, streaming on Netflix.

title: Authorities allege man threatened officers with knife before fatal shooting in Westminster
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A 22-year-old man was fatally shot by Westminster police after allegedly threatening them with a knife.
The incident happened on South Ashburnham Road as officers served an arrest warrant around 4:40 p.m.
State Police detectives and Westminster police are investigating; officers involved are on administrative leave.

title: Rachel Dratch has a hairdresser to thank for viral ‘Celebrity Jeopardy!’ moment
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Rachel Dratch's reaction to a 'Celebrity Jeopardy! All Stars' clue about the Spanish Armada went viral, earning millions of views.
Dratch attributed her surprising knowledge of the Spanish Armada to a conversation with hairdresser Eddie Cook before the show.
Despite an early exit from the tournament, Dratch's viral moment was widely shared on social media, gaining significant attention.

title: She survived a brain aneurysm — now she’s finishing the Marathon Majors in Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Andrea Calleja, 46, will finish the World Marathon Majors at the 2026 Boston Marathon after surviving a brain aneurysm.
Calleja started her marathon journey at the 2017 New York City Marathon and survived a ruptured brain aneurysm in 2019.
She volunteers with the Brain Aneurysm Foundation to support others and has completed marathons in Berlin, Chicago, Tokyo, London, and Sydney.

title: Pop-up bar dedicated to women’s sports now open in Boston
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 261 at Para Maria, a pop-up bar focused on women's sports, is open at The Envoy Hotel in Boston.
The bar features a women’s sports history photo exhibit and a menu honoring Boston Marathon pioneer Kathrine Switzer.
A portion of cocktail sales supports Dream Big!, aiding girls from low-income households in sports involvement.
